_description: Firecrawl web scraping, search, and crawling flows
_gotchas: scrape returns markdown by default (best for LLM consumption)
crawl is ASYNC - returns job ID, poll with crawl-status
Cloudflare sites may need waitFor param in milliseconds
search costs 1 credit per result returned
map is cheapest way to discover URLs before selective scraping

_scrape_single_page._description: Scrape a URL and get markdown content. Best for reading a specific page.

_scrape_cloudflare_site._description: For Cloudflare-protected or JS-heavy sites, add waitFor
_scrape_cloudflare_site._note: waitFor is milliseconds. 3000 = 3 seconds. Costs same credits but takes longer.

_search._description: Search the web and get markdown content for each result. Like Serper + scrape in one call.
_search._curl: curl -s -X POST 'https://api.firecrawl.dev/v1/search' -H 'Authorization: Bearer $FIRECRAWL_API_KEY' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query": "SaaS event venues Austin TX", "limit": 5}'
_search._tool: firecrawl.sh search --q 'SaaS event venues Austin TX' --limit 5

_crawl_site._description: Crawl a site starting from a URL. ASYNC operation.

_map_site._description: Fast sitemap/URL extraction. No content, just URLs. Cheapest operation.
_map_site._use_case: Discover all pages on a site, then selectively scrape the interesting ones

_escalation_pattern.tier_0: Serper search (1 credit) - find URLs, get snippets
_escalation_pattern.tier_1: Firecrawl map (cheap) - discover all pages on a domain
_escalation_pattern.tier_2: Firecrawl scrape (1 credit) - read specific pages as markdown
_escalation_pattern.tier_3: Firecrawl crawl (1 credit/page) - read entire sites
